frame_analyses.description: Trainee preparing instruments outside the trainer box
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Pre-task preparation, instruments visible but not yet in field

frame_analyses.description: First instrument enters the field, beginning needle loading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Timer starts, needle driver visible entering workspace

frame_analyses.description: Needle loaded, positioning for first mark placement on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle orientation, can see the slit Penrose drain with marks

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through first mark on the drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Accurate placement through the mark, proper needle trajectory

frame_analyses.description: Completing suture passage through both marks
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second mark placement appears accurate, suture now through both marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw of surgeon's knot with right hand
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Starting knot tying sequence, appears to be forming double throw
Double throw formation visible, using right hand primarily

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being tied with left hand
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching executed, now using left hand for single throw
Good tension applied, knot appears secure

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being tied with right hand
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switched back to right, completing final single throw
Knot appears well-formed and properly positioned

frame_analyses.description: Task completed, final view of secured knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Drain appears well-closed with no visible gap

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw executed well
Good hand switching to left hand for single throw
Proper hand switch back to right hand for final throw

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Excellent closure with no visible gap in the drain slit

technique_summary: Excellent execution of Task 5 with proper surgeon's knot formation, correct hand switching between throws, and accurate suture placement. The knot sequence was performed correctly and the drain was well-closed.
improvement_suggestions: Continue practicing to maintain this excellent technique
Focus on slight speed improvements while maintaining accuracy
strengths: Perfect knot sequence with surgeon's knot first throw
Efficient movement and good instrument control
